title: phyllanthus angolensis
intro: Phyllanthus angolensis, also known as Bushman's poison or Marum, is a plant species native to southern Africa, characterized by its woody stem and small white flowers. It has been found to have various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits. The plant has been traditionally used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and snake bites, as well as to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, Phyllanthus angolensis can be consumed as a tea or used as a tincture to leverage its medicinal and health benefits.
